features.name: Dimensionality Reduction
features.description: Reduces high-dimensional tabular data to a small number of components via PCA and multi-column projection, with preprocessing adapters for numeric and string columns and an optional UMAP/t-SNE pipeline.
features.evidence_summary: func:EDA:PCA links directly to /help/explore/dim-reduction#pca; the preprocessing functions carry the dim-red-preprocessing-function role tag, and Multi Column Dimensionality Reduction is the generic wrapper for the whole pipeline.

features.name: DBSCAN Clustering
features.description: Density-based spatial clustering of applications with noise (DBSCAN), available as a standalone function on a dataframe and as a post-processing step wired into the dimensionality-reduction pipeline.
features.evidence_summary: Both functions share the DBSCAN name; one is a standalone clustering function and the other is tagged dim-red-postprocessing-function, forming two entry points for the same algorithm.

features.name: MCL Clustering
features.description: Markov Clustering (MCL) groups dataset rows by simulating stochastic flow on a graph, exposed as a compute function, a configurable viewer, and a parameter-editor panel.
features.evidence_summary: All three functions share the MCL name root; MCL has role=viewer, MCLClustering is the compute entry point, and GetMCLEditor (role=editor) provides the parameter UI—a canonical viewer+editor pair.

features.name: PLS / Multivariate Analysis
features.description: Partial Least Squares (PLS) regression and multivariate analysis (MVA) that identifies latent factors and builds a linear predictive model maximally correlated with a response variable.
features.evidence_summary: PLS, topMenuPLS, multivariateAnalysis, MVA demo, and the trainPLSRegression adapter family all describe PLS/MVA in their descriptions; changelog entries v1.4.2 and v1.5.1 confirm ongoing MVA and PLS quadratic regression development.

features.name: SVM Classification
features.description: Support Vector Machine (SVM) binary and multi-class classification with four kernel variants—Linear, RBF, Polynomial, and Sigmoid—each exposing train, apply, applicability-check, interactive-check, and visualization adapters.
features.evidence_summary: All 20 functions share the SVM name suffix, follow an identical train/apply/isApplicable/isInteractive/visualize adapter pattern, and differ only by kernel type—four parallel implementations of the same classifier interface.

features.name: ANOVA
features.description: One-way analysis of variance that determines whether a categorical factor has a statistically significant effect on a numeric column, presenting an F-test result with tabbed result views.
features.evidence_summary: Single function explicitly named ANOVA; changelog v1.4.6 describes F-test description update and tabbed results UI, confirming it as a distinct user-facing analysis.

features.name: KNN Missing Value Imputation
features.description: Fills missing values in numeric columns using the k-nearest neighbors algorithm, available as both a column-level dialog and a whole-table batch operation.
features.evidence_summary: Both functions share the 'KNN impute' name and describe missing-value imputation via KNN; a dedicated help page confirms this as a documented user-facing capability.

features.name: Linear Regression
features.description: Ordinary least-squares linear regression model adapter with train, apply, and applicability-check functions for use in the platform's predictive modelling pipeline.
features.evidence_summary: Four functions share the LinearRegression suffix following the platform's standard train/apply/isApplicable/isInteractive ML adapter pattern.

features.name: Softmax (Logistic) Classification
features.description: Multi-class softmax classifier adapter with train, apply, and applicability-check functions for use in the platform's predictive modelling pipeline.
features.evidence_summary: Four functions share the Softmax suffix following the platform's standard train/apply/isApplicable/isInteractive ML adapter pattern.

features.name: XGBoost
features.description: Gradient-boosted tree ensemble (XGBoost) model adapter with train, apply, and applicability-check functions for use in the platform's predictive modelling pipeline.
features.evidence_summary: Four functions share the XGBooster suffix following the platform's standard ML adapter pattern; changelog v1.4.9 references an ML library update that includes XGBoost.

features.name: Pareto Front / Multi-Objective Optimization
features.description: Identifies Pareto-optimal rows across conflicting numeric objectives and displays the trade-off frontier in a dedicated interactive Pareto front viewer.
features.evidence_summary: Both functions share the 'Pareto front' name (one is the app, one is a viewer role); changelog v1.4.11 explicitly describes the simultaneous release of the viewer and the optimization app.

features.name: Probabilistic Multi-Parameter Optimization (pMPO)
features.description: Trains probabilistic MPO desirability models from numeric or categorical columns and exports them as MPO profiles, with an interactive app UI and synthetic dataset generator.
features.evidence_summary: All three functions share the Pmpo name root; changelog v1.4.12 and v1.5.0 describe the pMPO training app, dataset generation, and model export as a single evolving feature.
